questions.question: What happens when a subclass provides a different implementation of a method than its superclass?
Which of the following methods allows an object to invoke a method on its own class without knowing the class type at compile-time?
What is the benefit of using an abstract class in Java?
options: Abstract classes can only have static methods
Abstract classes can provide a blueprint for other classes to follow
Abstract classes can only have private methods

questions.question: Which of the following keywords is used to indicate that a class cannot be subclassed?

Java provides a feature called Local Variable Type Inference (var keyword) which allows you to initialize variables without specifying their type. Is this feature related to any of the following concepts?
questions.explanation: Local Variable Type Inference and Inheritance
